channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Democratic lawmakers from Connecticut report Thanksgiving bomb threats against their homes
item.description: At least five Democratic members of Congress from Connecticut have been targeted by bomb threats on their homes.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The outlook is uncertain for AI regulations as the US government pivots to full Republican control
item.description: The federal government is about to transition from one that prioritized safeguards for artificial intelligence to one more focused on eliminating red tape.
item.dc:creator: Dan Merica And Ali Swenson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 7 most competitive Senate races in 2026
item.description: The race for key Senate contests in 2026 is already getting underway, with a few major potential candidates hinting they may join the race.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zuckerberg dines with Trump in Mar-a-Lago
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Donald Trump dined on Wednesday with Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg at the president-elect’s Mar-a-Lago club in Florida, bringing together the Facebook founder and the former president who was once banned from that social network.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Homan taking death threats against him 'more seriously' after Trump officials targeted with violent threats
item.description: Incoming Trump border czar Tom Homan says his family has received death threats in the past and that the recent threats against Trump officials means he now takes them more seriously.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris never led Trump, internal polls showed — but DNC officials were kept in the dark
item.description: A top adviser to Kamala Harris said internal campaign polls never saw her beating Trump, despite her public polling posture.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scott County official says 20 absentee ballots in close House race were likely tossed
item.description: The missing ballots are at the heart of a court challenge from Minnesota House Republicans who have said a 14-vote win for a DFLer there is clouded. The race will dictate House control.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: End of Trump prosecutions renews scrutiny of DOJ's pace
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith's move to dismiss Donald Trump's two federal indictments has sparked finger-pointing from those eager to see the president-elect held to account, with critics airing their frustrations at the Department of Justice (DOJ) and the courts for the anticlimactic end to the case.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: 4 takeaways from Trump's health agency nominations
item.description: Almost all have been highly critical of the agencies they would run if confirmed, and experts say the nominees represent a complete overhaul of the vision and priorities of key agencies for health care and public health.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump-backed Florida official resigns, announces run for Gaetz seat
item.description: Florida’s Chief Financial Officer Jimmy Patronis resigned from his position and announced his candidacy for former Rep. Matt Gaetz's (R-Fla.) seat in the 1st Congressional District on Monday. In a letter addressed to Florida Gov.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: After delay, Trump signs agreement with Biden White House to begin formal transition handoff
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday reached a required agreement with President Joe Biden’s White House to allow his transition staff to coordinate with the existing federal workforce before taking office on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cheap Ozempic? How millions of Americans with obesity may get access to costly weight-loss drugs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Millions of obese Americans would get access to popular weekly injectables that would help them shed pounds quickly if a $ 35 billion proposal from the Biden administration is blessed by President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump 'border czar' willing to 'jail' Denver mayor over deportation position
item.description: "But, look, me and the Denver mayor, we agree on one thing. He’s willing to go to jail, I’m willing to put him in jail.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs MOU with Biden White House for next phase of transition
item.description: After a lengthy delay, President-elect Trump has signed off on a memorandum of understanding (MOU) with the White House, which will allow officials to meet with counterparts at departments and agencies ahead of the January transition of power.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here's where Trump's criminal cases stand
item.description: President-elect Trump’s legal strategy of delay is paying off, with his election victory changing the trajectory of his four criminal prosecutions.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Trump has promised lower taxes. These are 3 ways it could impact you
item.description: From "no tax on tips" to Social Security, Donald Trump has made a range of promises about the taxes Americans will pay. We take a look at three changes we could see in the months ahead.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Wamsley, MPR News

item.title: Walmart becomes latest — and biggest — company to roll back its DEI policies
item.description: Conservative groups have filed lawsuits against workplace diversity initiatives that prioritize historically marginalized groups.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: State bar counsel filed 2 complaints against Clark County Commissioner Justin Jones
item.description: The office of the state bar counsel, in its effort to discipline Clark County Commissioner Justin Jones, filed a complaint in February 2024 and another in October where it alleges Jones violated several rules of professional conduct, according to documents the 8 News Now Investigators have obtained.
item.dc:creator: Kyle J. Paine, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump threatens Canada, Mexico, China with tariffs
item.description: He said the countries weren't doing enough to control the flow of illegal drugs, especially fentanyl, and migrants across the border.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: New law forces landlords to notify tenants of bed bug infestations in NY
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A new law in New York requires landlords to notify tenants of bed bugs within three days of an infestation. However, landlords are only required to inform renters near the infestation and those directly at risk.
item.dc:creator: Julie Millet, PIX 11

item.title: How NYC congestion pricing tolls could impact drivers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York Gov. Kathy Hochul recently introduced a new plan to toll drivers who enter Manhattan below 60th Street. The revised congestion pricing plan would cost most drivers $9 during peak hours and $2.25 during off-peak hours. It's expected to go into effect in January.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11
